name: read_file
title: Read File
description: Reads the given file or a chunk of it. Generally, symbolic operations like find_symbol or find_referencing_symbols should be preferred if you know which symbols you are looking for. Returns the full text of the file at the given relative path.
why_use_it: Required before any line-level edit (replace_lines, delete_lines, insert_at_line) to confirm the exact content at the target lines.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: The relative path to the file to read.

start_line.title: Start Line
start_line.description: The 0-based index of the first line to be retrieved.

end_line.title: End Line
end_line.description: The 0-based index of the last line to be retrieved (inclusive). If None, read until the end of the file.

max_answer_chars.title: Max Answer Chars
max_answer_chars.description: If the file (chunk) is longer than this number of characters, no content will be returned. Don't adjust unless there is really no other way to get the content required for the task.

name: create_text_file
title: Create Text File
description: Write a new file or overwrite an existing file. If the enable_human_verification set to true (default is false), the content will be sent to the user for verification. The user can then accept, edit, or reject it. Only set this to true if EXPLICITLY requested by the user. Returns a message indicating success or failure.
why_use_it: The only tool that can write a file from scratch. Also the right choice when the changes to an existing file are so large that editing is harder than rewriting.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: The relative path to the file to create.

content.title: Content
content.description: The (appropriately encoded) content to write to the file.

enable_human_verification.title: Enable Human Verification
enable_human_verification.description: Whether to enable human in the loop or not.

name: list_dir
title: List Dir
description: Lists files and directories in the given directory (optionally with recursion). The following paths are always ignored: node_modules/, .venv/, .git, .next, .actovator, and any files matching .env*. Returns a JSON object with the names of directories and files within the given directory.
why_use_it: Gives you the project's folder structure so you can navigate with intention rather than guessing file locations.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: The relative path to the directory to list; pass '.' to scan the project root.

skip_ignored_files.title: Skip Ignored Files
skip_ignored_files.description: Whether to skip files and directories that are ignored by the project settings.

max_answer_chars.title: Max Answer Chars
max_answer_chars.description: If the output is longer than this number of characters, no content will be returned.

name: find_file
title: Find File
description: Finds non-gitignored files matching the given file mask within the given relative path. Returns a JSON object with the list of matching files.
why_use_it: Faster than listing directories when you know the filename or extension but not its location.

file_mask.title: File Mask
file_mask.description: The filename or file mask (using the wildcards * or ?) to search for.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: The relative path to the directory to search in; pass '.' to scan the project root.

name: replace_content
title: Replace Content
description: Replaces one or more occurrences of a given pattern in a file with new content. This is the preferred way to replace content in a file whenever the symbol-level tools are not appropriate. The regex mode allows very large sections of code to be replaced without fully quoting them. Returns a message indicating success or failure.
why_use_it: The most versatile editing tool short of symbolic operations. Regex mode can replace large sections without quoting every line, using .*? wildcards.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: The relative path to the file.

needle.title: Needle
needle.description: The string or regex pattern to search for.

repl.title: Repl
repl.description: The replacement string (verbatim). If mode is 'regex', the string can contain backreferences using the syntax $!1, $!2, etc.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Either 'literal' or 'regex', specifying how the needle parameter is to be interpreted.

allow_multiple_occurrences.title: Allow Multiple Occurrences
allow_multiple_occurrences.description: If True, the regex may match multiple occurrences in the file and all of them will be replaced.

name: delete_lines
title: Delete Lines
description: Deletes the given lines in the file. Requires that the same range of lines was previously read using the read_file tool to verify correctness of the operation.
why_use_it: Clean removal of dead code, unused imports, or deprecated blocks without leaving empty space.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: The relative path to the file.

start_line.title: Start Line
start_line.description: The 0-based index of the first line to be deleted.

end_line.title: End Line
end_line.description: The 0-based index of the last line to be deleted.

name: replace_lines
title: Replace Lines
description: Replaces the given range of lines in the given file. Requires that the same range of lines was previously read using the read_file tool to verify correctness of the operation.
why_use_it: Direct and unambiguous when you know exactly which lines to swap — useful for small, self-contained blocks.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: The relative path to the file.

start_line.title: Start Line
start_line.description: The 0-based index of the first line to be replaced.

end_line.title: End Line
end_line.description: The 0-based index of the last line to be replaced.

name: insert_at_line
title: Insert At Line
description: Inserts the given content at the given line in the file, pushing existing content of the line down. In general, symbolic insert operations like insert_after_symbol or insert_before_symbol should be preferred if you know which symbol you are looking for.
why_use_it: Useful for small, targeted insertions inside a function body when you can't use symbolic insert tools.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: The relative path to the file.

line.title: Line
line.description: The 0-based index of the line to insert content at.

name: search_for_pattern
title: Search For Pattern
description: Offers a flexible search for arbitrary patterns in the codebase, including the possibility to search in non-code files. Symbolic operations like find_symbol or find_referencing_symbols should be preferred if you know which symbols you are looking for. Returns a mapping of file paths to lists of matched consecutive lines.
why_use_it: Finds content when you don't know the exact symbol name — config values, string literals, comments, TODOs, or cross-cutting patterns spanning non-code files.

substring_pattern.title: Substring Pattern
substring_pattern.description: Regular expression for a substring pattern to search for.

context_lines_before.title: Context Lines Before
context_lines_before.description: Number of lines of context to include before each match.

context_lines_after.title: Context Lines After
context_lines_after.description: Number of lines of context to include after each match.

paths_include_glob.title: Paths Include Glob
paths_include_glob.description: Optional glob pattern specifying files to include in the search.

paths_exclude_glob.title: Paths Exclude Glob
paths_exclude_glob.description: Optional glob pattern specifying files to exclude from the search. Takes precedence over paths_include_glob.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: Only subpaths of this path will be analyzed. The path must exist, otherwise a FileNotFoundError is raised.

restrict_search_to_code_files.title: Restrict Search To Code Files
restrict_search_to_code_files.description: Whether to restrict the search to only those files where analyzed code symbols can be found.

max_answer_chars.title: Max Answer Chars
max_answer_chars.description: If the output is longer than this number of characters, no content will be returned. Make a stricter query instead of raising this limit.

name: restart_language_server
title: Restart Language Server
description: Use this tool only on explicit user request or after confirmation. It may be necessary to restart the language server if it hangs.
why_use_it: Recovers a hung or unresponsive language server so symbolic tools work again — without losing any file changes.

name: active_language_server
title: Active Language Server
description: Activates the language server for the project's programming languages. The first language is the default and will be used as a fallback. Special requirements: csharp requires a .sln file; pascal requires Free Pascal Compiler.
why_use_it: Without it, every symbolic tool (find_symbol, rename_symbol, etc.) is unavailable. All other tools still work, but you lose semantic understanding of the code.

languages.title: Languages
languages.description: The programming languages to be used in the project.

name: get_symbols_overview
title: Get Symbols Overview
description: Use this tool to get a high-level understanding of the code symbols in a file. This should be the first tool to call when you want to understand a new file, unless you already know what you are looking for. Returns a JSON object containing symbols grouped by kind in a compact format.
why_use_it: Gives you a mental map of an unknown file in one call. Avoids reading thousands of lines just to understand structure.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: The relative path to the file to get the overview of.

depth.title: Depth
depth.description: Depth up to which descendants of top-level symbols shall be retrieved (e.g. 1 retrieves immediate children). Default 0.

max_answer_chars.title: Max Answer Chars
max_answer_chars.description: If the overview is longer than this number of characters, no content will be returned.

name: find_symbol
title: Find Symbol
description: Retrieves information on all symbols/code entities (classes, methods, etc.) based on the given name path pattern. A name path is a path in the symbol tree within a source file. Returns a list of symbols (with locations) matching the name.
why_use_it: Pinpoints exactly what you need without reading unrelated code. Works across the whole codebase if needed.

depth.title: Depth
depth.description: Depth up to which descendants shall be retrieved. Default 0.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: Optional. Restrict search to this file or directory. If None, searches entire codebase.

include_body.title: Include Body
include_body.description: Whether to include the symbol's source code. Use judiciously.

include_info.title: Include Info
include_info.description: Whether to include additional info (hover-like, typically including docstring and signature).

include_kinds.title: Include Kinds
include_kinds.description: List of LSP symbol kind integers to include. If not provided, all kinds are included.

exclude_kinds.title: Exclude Kinds
exclude_kinds.description: Optional. List of LSP symbol kind integers to exclude. Takes precedence over include_kinds.

substring_matching.title: Substring Matching
substring_matching.description: If True, use substring matching for the last element of the pattern.

max_answer_chars.title: Max Answer Chars
max_answer_chars.description: Max characters for the JSON result. If exceeded, no content is returned.

name: find_referencing_symbols
title: Find Referencing Symbols
description: Finds references to the symbol at the given name_path. The result will contain metadata about the referencing symbols as well as a short code snippet around the reference. Returns a list of JSON objects with the symbols referencing the requested symbol.
why_use_it: Prevents breaking changes. Before renaming or modifying a symbol, you know exactly how many places depend on it and where.

name_path.title: Name Path
name_path.description: For finding the symbol to find references for, same logic as in the find_symbol tool.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: The relative path to the file containing the symbol for which to find references. Must be a file, not a directory.

include_info.title: Include Info
include_info.description: Whether to include additional info about the referencing symbols; can be slow depending on the language.

include_kinds.title: Include Kinds
include_kinds.description: Same as in the find_symbol tool.

exclude_kinds.title: Exclude Kinds
exclude_kinds.description: Same as in the find_symbol tool.

max_answer_chars.title: Max Answer Chars
max_answer_chars.description: Same as in the find_symbol tool.

name: replace_symbol_body
title: Replace Symbol Body
description: Replaces the body of the symbol with the given name_path. The tool shall be used to replace symbol bodies that have been previously retrieved (e.g. via find_symbol). Do not use this tool if you do not know what exactly constitutes the body of the symbol.
why_use_it: Safer and more semantic than line-level edits. Operates on a named symbol rather than fragile line numbers.

name_path.title: Name Path
name_path.description: For finding the symbol to replace, same logic as in the find_symbol tool.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: The relative path to the file containing the symbol.

body.title: Body
body.description: The new symbol body including the signature line. Does NOT include preceding docstrings, comments, or imports.

name: insert_after_symbol
title: Insert After Symbol
description: Inserts the given body/content after the end of the definition of the given symbol (via the symbol's location). A typical use case is to insert a new class, function, method, field or variable assignment.
why_use_it: Avoids hardcoding line numbers. Anchors the insertion to a semantic position that survives other edits.

name_path.title: Name Path
name_path.description: Name path of the symbol after which to insert content.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: The relative path to the file containing the symbol.

body.title: Body
body.description: The body/content to be inserted. The inserted code shall begin with the next line after the symbol.

name: insert_before_symbol
title: Insert Before Symbol
description: Inserts the given content before the beginning of the definition of the given symbol (via the symbol's location). A typical use case is to insert a new class, function, method, field or variable assignment; or a new import statement before the first symbol in the file.
why_use_it: Same anchor stability as insert_after_symbol, but places code before. Useful for imports and leading declarations.

name_path.title: Name Path
name_path.description: Name path of the symbol before which to insert content.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: The relative path to the file containing the symbol.

body.title: Body
body.description: The body/content to be inserted before the line in which the referenced symbol is defined.

name: rename_symbol
title: Rename Symbol
description: Renames the symbol with the given name_path to new_name throughout the entire codebase. For languages with method overloading like Java, name_path may have to include a method's signature to uniquely identify a method. Returns result summary indicating success or failure.
why_use_it: The only safe way to rename. Manual find-and-replace misses cross-file references and can break string literals unintentionally.

name_path.title: Name Path
name_path.description: Name path of the symbol to rename.

relative_path.title: Relative Path
relative_path.description: The relative path to the file containing the symbol to rename.

new_name.title: New Name
new_name.description: The new name for the symbol.

name: execute_shell_command
title: Execute Shell Command
description: Execute a shell command and return its output. If there is a memory about suggested commands, read that first. Never execute unsafe shell commands. Do not use this tool to start long-running processes or processes that require user interaction. Returns a JSON object containing the command's stdout and optionally stderr output.
why_use_it: Lets you verify changes are correct by running tests, linters, build tools, or type checkers immediately after edits.

cwd.title: Cwd
cwd.description: The working directory to execute the command in. If None, the project root will be used.

capture_stderr.title: Capture Stderr
capture_stderr.description: Whether to capture and return stderr output.

max_answer_chars.title: Max Answer Chars
max_answer_chars.description: If the output is longer than this number of characters, no content will be returned.
